title: In My Kitchen: Where Healing Meets Hustle
excerpt: This isn't just meal prep. This is how I stay disciplined, healthy, and in control of my life.

sections.heading: The Philosophy
sections.content: I don't cook just to eat-I cook to live better. Everything in my kitchen has purpose. Food is how I control my energy, my health, and my day. I eat to live, I don't live to eat.

sections.heading: My Non-Negotiables
sections.content: No plain white salt-if I use salt, it's pink Himalayan. I balance everything. If I eat carbs, I adjust something else. If I eat meat, I limit sugar. I cook in bulk because eating at home is healthier and cheaper. And yes-clean as you go.

sections.heading: The Science
sections.content: Meal prepping can reduce calorie intake by up to 30%. Eating leafy greens supports heart health and lowers disease risk. Drinking water supports metabolism. Cooking at home consistently lowers added sugar and unhealthy fat compared with relying on takeout.

sections.heading: How I Cook
sections.content: A good cook can walk into a kitchen, look around, and make something out of what's there. I don't rely on recipes-I rely on instinct and understanding how foods work together.

sections.heading: Staples
sections.content: Kale, beets, and coconut water are part of my lifestyle. I also grow what I can. Fresh food around me keeps me creative and consistent.

sections.heading: Final Word
sections.content: This kitchen is where I reset. Where I make decisions that protect my health and my future. Take control of your kitchen, and you take control of your life.
